mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 100k signals robust labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in months with PAYEMS > 100k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which drives equity valuations higher through improved corporate revenue prospects over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with concurrent HOUST/RSAFS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 115 signals manufacturing weakness, which reduces corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO decline periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03 in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, which increases bond yields and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPI > 2.5% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03 in inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1) below 1% signals economic weakness, which reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in GDP contraction periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03 in recessionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% indicates tight labor market conditions, which supports wage growth and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in UNRATE < 5% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's Rank IC < 0.05 in tight labor market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 120 signals strong manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03 in expansionary regimes.
